folio.label: 71r
folio.l: ñe shuln haue queynte rigút I now , at eeue
He is to gret a nygard , that wol werne
A man to ligút a candele , at his lanterne
He shal haue , neuäe the lasse ligút , ¹dee
Haue thow I now , the thar nat pleyne thee
Thow seist´ also , if that we make vs gay
Witú clothynge , or witú precious array
That it is ¹ile , of oure chastitee
And ©et witú sorwe , æè must enforce the
And seyn theise wordes , in the Aposteles name
In habyt mad , witú chastite and shame
ñe wommen , shulde apparaille ©ow q¶ he
And nat in tressed heer , and riche perre
As peerles ne gold , ne clothes riche
After thy text , ne after thy rubriche
I wol nat werche , as meche as dotú a gnat
Thow seist als , I walke out lyke a kat
For who so wolde senge , the Cattes skyn
Than wolde the kat , dwellen in his In
And if the kattes skyn , be slyke and gay
She wol nat dwelle , in house half a day
But fortú she wol , or any day be dawed
To shewe hire skyn , & go on Caterwawed
This is to seyn , if I be gay sire shrewe
I wol renne out , my borel for to shewe
Sire olde foole , what helpetú the to aspyen
Thougú thow preye Argus , w his hundred eyen
To be my wardecors , as he can best
In feitú he shal nat kepe me , but me list
ñet coude I make his berd , also mote I thee
Thow seidest eke , æat there ben thynges three
The whiche thynges , troublen al this erthe
And that no wygút , may endure the ferthe
O leue sire shrewe , Iúc short thy lyf
ñet precest æè and seist , an hateful wyf
I rekened is , for oon of theise meschaunces
Betú there noone othere , resemblaunces
That ©e may likne , ©oure parables to
But if a seely wyf , be on of thoo
Thow likenest eke , wom~annes loue to helle
To bareyn lond , there water may nat dwelle
Thow likenest it also , to wylde fyre
The more it brennetú , the more it hatú desire
To consume euäy thyngö , that wol brent be
